D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Opal — Silica Sphere Bragg Lattice

opal is hydrated silica with ~250 nm spheres self-assembled into a close-packed lattice; viewing angle shifts the Bragg condition and the stone flashes a different colour

Precious opal is built of monodisperse silica spheres (200-350 nm) self-assembled into a face-centred cubic lattice. The (111) plane spacing satisfies Bragg's law at visible wavelengths; rotate the stone and the spacing in your line-of-sight changes, sweeping the flash colour. This is the "play of colour" — pure structural, no pigments.

Drag viewing angle. The flashed colour sweeps red → green → blue as the effective lattice spacing shortens with angle.
